catch_up_note: This is a BACKDATED CATCH-UP scan executed 2026-07-19 to reconstruct the 2026-07-18 nightly scan. The evidence window was hard-capped at 2026-07-18 inclusive (lookback 2026-07-04 to 2026-07-18); any item published or dated 2026-07-19 or later was excluded from scoring/evidence. Four items explicitly deferred by the 07-17 cycle (Iran cumulative-toll/MoU-suspension, Afghanistan HRW hunger-crisis report, Myanmar resistance-notes update, and Meta's Jul 27 bellwether trial) were revisited: the first three are now confirmed in-window and processed as evidence below; Meta's Jul 27 trial date remains forward-looking and stays deferred/out of window, unchanged.

top_entities.name: Pakistan
top_entities.news_summary: OPERATION SHAABAN TOLL CONTINUES CLIMBING (WITHIN WINDOW, SUSTAINED/CONTINUING): Security-source reporting puts the dedicated Operation Shaaban toll variably at 71-123 depending on the tally cited, with combined operation-plus-IBO totals still climbing since the Jul 4-8 coordinated insurgent assaults that killed 42 (38 security personnel, 4 civilians). No newly-dated mass-civilian-harm event surfaced this cycle beyond the ongoing daily militant-death drumbeat; casualties remain overwhelmingly reported as militants, entirely security-source-reported and unverified by independent monitors, now into a fourth consecutive week.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: DEFERRED ITEM NOW IN-WINDOW: IRAN REPORTS CUMULATIVE TOLL OF 50+ KILLED, 500+ INJURED SINCE JUL 6; TEHRAN DECLARES US-IRAN MoU 'SUSPENDED' (dated 2026-07-18, verified against Al Jazeera/PressTV/CENTCOM-adjacent reporting, interstate/US-attributable): Iran's Health Ministry reported 12 additional deaths in the last day, raising the cumulative toll since Jul 6 to 50 killed (incl. 5 women, 2 children) and 500+ injured (incl. 32 women, 18 children), following a seventh consecutive night of US strikes. A desalination plant strike left 10,000 without water. Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi said the US has 'violated and suspended all its commitments' under the Islamabad MoU. Two US service members were separately reported killed and one missing after an Iranian attack in Jordan (CENTCOM), which triggered further US strikes. ATTRIBUTION HOLD MAINTAINED: this harm is interstate/US-attributable, not scored as an Iran own-conduct debit; Iran's own record (784+ executions YTD) already prices the near-floor 2.5. This item was explicitly deferred from the 07-17 cycle and is now confirmed in-window.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: GAZA STRIKES CONTINUE JUL 17-18 — CUMULATIVE POST-CEASEFIRE TOLL CLIMBS FURTHER (within window, NEW dated incidents): Israeli strikes in Gaza City on Jul 18 killed at least 9 Palestinians including 3 children — a strike on an apartment in Nasr neighborhood killed 5 (children aged 8-18), and a separate strike on a Zeitoun gathering killed 4. This follows the Jul 17 funeral strike in Nuseirat (7 killed, 22 wounded) already logged. Israel's military said it targeted Hamas infrastructure/militants without elaborating. Floor sustained and reinforced by fresh dated mass-casualty incidents on consecutive days.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: EL OBEID SIEGE CONTINUES — NO RESOLUTION, RSF CONTROLS ALL ROUTES EXCEPT EAST (within window, sustained/continuing): ~500,000 residents plus 105,000 displaced remain trapped; OHCHR's documented 15 drone strikes/45+ civilian deaths over three weeks remains the operative figure, food prices still up ~300%. No new dated mass-casualty spike beyond what was already logged 07-17; UN continues warning of an 'impending catastrophe' comparable to El Fasher. Floor sustained; cannot move lower.

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.news_summary: SUSTAINED CIVILIAN-CASUALTY PATTERN CONTINUES; NOTE — TODAY'S MOST SIGNIFICANT DATED INCIDENT IS UKRAINIAN-ATTRIBUTED, NOT RUSSIAN OWN-CONDUCT (within window, sustained; attribution discipline maintained): Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian territory (Kotovsk warehouse, Moscow region, Belgorod) killed 9 and wounded 60-80+ on Jul 18 — this harm is Ukraine's own conduct under the acting-party rule (see Ukraine record) and is NOT logged as a Russian own-conduct debit. No newly-dated Russian strike on Ukrainian civilians surfaced independently this cycle beyond the previously-logged Jul 15 Sumy glide-bomb strike; Russia's floor rests on its own persistent conduct record (systematic strikes, torture, ICC intimidation) and cannot move lower regardless.

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: ATTRIBUTION-RELEVANT FLAG FOR ASSESSOR: UKRAINIAN DRONE STRIKES ON RUSSIAN LOGISTICS SITES KILLED CIVILIAN WORKERS (dated 2026-07-18, own-conduct consideration): Ukrainian drones hit warehouses (incl. two Wildberries retail warehouses), fuel depots, and maritime targets across 19 Russian regions overnight; Russian officials report 9 killed and 60-80+ wounded, including 7 civilian night-shift workers killed at a Kotovsk warehouse in Tambov region. Under the acting-party attribution rule this is Ukraine's own conduct causing civilian harm on Russian soil — a genuinely new consideration distinct from the routine 'Russian strikes on Ukraine attributed to Russia' framing that has dominated prior cycles, and worth flagging for the assessor's attention as a potential (not yet adjudicated) own-conduct data point. Separately, Russian strikes on Ukraine and narrowing Donetsk humanitarian access continue per the 07-17 record; no new dated Russian-attributed incident surfaced independently this cycle.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of C
top_entities.news_summary: EBOLA TOLL CONTINUES CLIMBING — 2,124+ CASES, 828-864 DEATHS DEPENDING ON TRACKER (data through Jul 15-16, within window, continuing, disease-attributed): DRC's Bundibugyo-strain outbreak remains the fastest-growing on record (2,000+ cases reached in under two months vs. 10+ months for the 2018-19 outbreak); WHO/ECDC note the true toll may be roughly double the official count. Disease-attributed; near-floor arithmetic forecloses any composite move regardless of severity. top_entities.name: Afghanistan
top_entities.news_summary: DEFERRED ITEM NOW IN-WINDOW, DATE-VERIFIED AGAINST PRIMARY SOURCE PER 07-17 RELIABILITY WARNING: HRW PUBLISHES 'I CAN'T FEED MY CHILDREN' HUNGER-CRISIS REPORT (verified hrw.org/news/2026/07/17/i-cant-feed-my-children — publication date confirmed directly from the HRW domain path, addressing last cycle's date-integrity concern): HRW documents that 17M+ Afghans face severe food insecurity in 2026 and ~22M require urgent food/health assistance; only 20% of Afghan households can afford sufficient food without aid (down from 28% pre-Oct-2025). The UN's $1.71B 2026 appeal is under 20% funded at midyear, compounded by the sharp cut in US humanitarian financing (previously ~40% of Afghanistan aid). CRITICALLY, this is not purely an external funding story: HRW attributes part of the access failure directly to Taliban policy — restrictions on women working in humanitarian/UN roles materially impede aid delivery to vulnerable households, an own-conduct component distinct from the funding shortfall. Net: this is the first item assessed next cycle per the 07-17 digest's instruction, and it carries a genuine (if partly externally-driven) own-conduct component.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW ENFORCEMENT CONTINUES — NO NEW DATED PRC ENFORCEMENT ACTION THIS CYCLE (within window, sustained): The Law on Promoting Ethnic Unity and Progress (in force since Jul 1) continues to draw UN High Commissioner and multi-government criticism, particularly over its extraterritorial reach into Taiwan and the diaspora; Xinjiang/Tibet are widely assessed as facing a legal capstone on already-existing assimilation policy. No newly-dated PRC enforcement or crackdown action was identified 2026-07-04 through 2026-07-18 beyond the already-known Jul 1 law taking effect.

top_entities.name: Venezuela
top_entities.news_summary: EARTHQUAKE DEATH TOLL RISES TO 5,119; IMF RELEASES EMERGENCY AID (dated 2026-07-18, disaster-attributed, sub-threshold): The Jun 24 twin-earthquake death toll climbed to 5,119 killed (16,740+ injured, 21,470+ in makeshift camps, 17,907 without housing) as of Jul 18 reporting, up materially from the ~3,800-5,000 range logged in prior cycles. The IMF released emergency aid amid the deepening political crisis the earthquake has intensified. Disaster-attributed; no new own-conduct escalation (aid diversion, responder denial) identified this cycle.

top_entities.name: Lebanon
top_entities.news_summary: CONTINUING LOW-INTENSITY EXCHANGES JUL 18 — NO MASS-CASUALTY EVENT, HARM MIXED-ATTRIBUTION (within window, continuing): The Israeli military reported striking a Hezbollah drone cell operating over southern Lebanon (externally-attributed, Israeli conduct); separately, a Lebanese Army soldier was killed and two wounded when a suspicious device — which the IDF says it did not plant, implying Hezbollah placement — exploded in an army vehicle in Mansouri. This is non-state-actor harm to Lebanese state personnel, not Lebanese state own-conduct. No new mass-casualty incident on the scale of the Jul 6 Nabatieh strike (already logged) surfaced this cycle.

top_entities.name: United States
top_entities.news_summary: MIXED SIGNAL: ICE ENFORCEMENT PACE HITS RECORD, DHS TIGHTENS STUDENT-VISA STAY RULES, BUT COURTS CONTINUE TO CHECK DETENTION VIA HABEAS RELIEF (within window, dated Jul 17-18, own-conduct with judicial-counterweight): DHS published a final rule (Jul 17) establishing a fixed admission period and extension procedure for nonimmigrant academic students, exchange visitors and foreign media representatives (effective Sep 15) — a tightening of a previously open-ended stay category. Separately, border czar Tom Homan said ICE recorded its highest-ever monthly arrest total in June, with July on pace to exceed it and deportations running ~40% above last year's pace; roughly 65,000 detainees have been released via court-ordered habeas and other judicial proceedings, a genuine judicial check on executive detention practice operating in parallel. No new mass-casualty or detention-death event was identified this specific cycle. United States remains a strong candidate for the next full priority-cluster assessment per the 07-17 digest's carried-forward flag.

top_entities.name: Cuba
top_entities.news_summary: GRID CRISIS AND POLITICAL-PRISONER RECORD BOTH REAFFIRMED, NO NEW ESCALATION THIS SPECIFIC CYCLE (within window, sustained): The Jul 14 third-blackout-in-10-days event (10M without power) remains the most recent dated grid failure; HRW's 700+ and Prisoners Defenders' 1,200+ political-prisoner counts are reaffirmed without a new updated figure. entity_reviews.name: United States
entity_reviews.summary: MIXED SIGNAL: ICE ENFORCEMENT PACE HITS RECORD, DHS TIGHTENS STUDENT-VISA STAY RULES, BUT COURTS CONTINUE TO CHECK DETENTION VIA HABEAS RELIEF (within window, dated Jul 17-18, own-conduct with judicial-counterweight): DHS published a final rule (Jul 17) establishing a fixed admission period and extension procedure for nonimmigrant academic students, exchange visitors and foreign media representatives (effective Sep 15) — a tightening of a previously open-ended stay category. Separately, border czar Tom Homan said ICE recorded its highest-ever monthly arrest total in June, with July on pace to exceed it and deportations running ~40% above last year's pace; roughly 65,000 detainees have been released via court-ordered habeas and other judicial proceedings, a genuine judicial check on executive detention practice operating in parallel. No new mass-casualty or detention-death event was identified this specific cycle. United States remains a strong candidate for the next full priority-cluster assessment per the 07-17 digest's carried-forward flag.
